message:
perf(timer): backward-slot guards, large-jump fast-path, and empty-wheel optimization in hierarchical timer wheel

TimerWheel::slot_for: Clamp computed tick to at least current_tick before
modding into slot index. Prevents timers with past deadlines from being placed
behind the cursor, which would delay them by a full wheel revolution until the
cursor wraps around. Now past-deadline timers fire on the very next tick.

HierarchicalTimerWheel::advance_to -- large jump fast-path (>65536 ticks):
Instead of O(ticks_skipped) individual tick() calls, drain all four levels
completely, fire expired timers, update cursors directly to target tick, and
re-insert remaining timers at their new slot positions. This is O(active_timers)
instead of O(ticks_to_advance), which is dramatically better when timers are
sparse but time gaps are large (e.g., system resume after long sleep).

HierarchicalTimerWheel::advance_to -- empty wheel fast-path: Inside the
tick-by-tick loop, check is_empty() and jump all four level cursors directly
to target_tick if no timers exist. Avoids pointless O(ticks) iteration when
the wheel has nothing to fire.

HierarchicalTimerWheel::slot_for_level: Same backward-slot guard as the flat
wheel. Compute current level tick from current_tick and clamp the deadline tick
to at least that value, preventing timers from being placed behind the level
cursor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
